comments.author: @Asparas-d7t
comments.text:
China is run by competent people who bring tangible benefits for people to enjoy. Its leaders are selected on delivery, not by vote based on fake promises. 
Officials have to start at the bottom of the ladder. They will never be promoted to a higher position if they haven't accomplished something significant that benefits the community. Upon taking office, they propose a five-year plan they must achieve. At the deadline, they are judged on their results. If they haven't met them, they stay at the same level, or even get replaced if they showed worse performance.
Let's  say someone starts as a village chief (village chiefs are elected by villagers in direct democracy). If he demonstrates leadership, it’s possible for him to enter the local party apparatus and start climbing the ladder. 
He can work his way up through township and county-level position, then becomes a county chief, a city mayor, prefect, governor, and so on.... He will never make it to Beijing, where the fate of the country is decided, if he hasn't proven his skills, based on his achievements, at every step. When he finally get there, his qualifications have been already proven by his extensive experience in accomplishing concrete projects. 
President Xi have spent three decades moving through rural, local and provincial roles before reaching the national level.
If some of our leaders were in China, they would remain village chiefs their entire lives. Their big fund raising and big promises to get elected are useless.

comments.author: @prototropo
comments.text:
I admire the amazing progress of the Chinese economy and statecraft. However, the paranoia that the CCP exerts on press freedoms and individual liberties will remain a pointless obstruction for Chinese society. 
      China has nothing to fear by ~ 
* allowing citizens to speak their mind, 
* to love & marry whom they wish, 
* to honor minority cultures and abandon punitive repression, and 
* to allow the pursuit of scientific truth under academic freedoms and ethical regulations.
